principles: Fresh build from the supplied script, not a patch of the previous generated deck.
Use the slide catalogue as a renderer vocabulary, not the creative ceiling.
Apply Amit style: short declarative sentences, sharp contrast, concrete operating stakes, and minimal em dash dependence.
Keep the Messi metaphor as a bookend: hook, outcome, and closing tagline only.
Use VEDA as proof of system design discipline, not as a technical detour.
Preserve visual variety by alternating narrative, proof, architecture, framework, roadmap, and CTA moments.

visual_diversity.note: Consecutive dark card families are allowed only where the narrative explicitly moves from problem to principle.

slides.creative_intent: Premium CXO opening that frames AI adoption as a system problem.
slides.visual_anchor: strong title block and logo field

slides.creative_intent: Use the Messi metaphor once as a key-person-risk hook.
Make prompt-first deployment failure feel like operating risk.
slides.visual_repeat_reason: Slides 2 and 3 form a hook-to-problem pair but differ in card count and narrative job.

slides.creative_intent: Show AI as an amplifier that moves mess from input to operating consequence.
slides.visual_anchor: risk flow from messy input to scaled consequence

slides.creative_intent: Reframe enterprise AI from another dashboard to a governed operating layer.
slides.visual_anchor: layered system stack: context, logic, QA, execution

slides.creative_intent: Introduce SHIFT as a failure-mode-aware executive framework.
Shift from argument to proof without adding visual clutter.
Show VEDA as an engineered build process with five design decisions.
Make VEDA look like a governed operating architecture, not a list of files.
Return to the Messi metaphor as the payoff: raise the floor, not just the ceiling.
Make the 12-month path concrete, sequenced, and executive-legible.
Close commercially while preserving the system argument and Messi tagline.
Place legal and proof-of-concept notes outside the selling narrative.
